steps.title: Use the rectangle area formula
steps.content: For a rectangle, area = length × width. With area 88 square meters and length 11 meters, set up $88 = 11w$ and solve for the width.

steps.title: Apply the rectangle area formula
steps.content: The area of a rectangle is found using Area = length × width. We know the area is 88 square meters and the length is 11 meters. Substituting these values into the formula: $$88 = 11 \times \text{width}$$

steps.title: Solve for width
steps.content: Divide both sides by 11 to isolate the width: $$\text{width} = \frac{88}{11} = 8$$ The width is 8 meters.
